steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $\frac{9qt}{q-5t}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Isolate s terms on one side
steps.content:
Starting with $18qrt - 2qrs + 10rst = 0$, move $18qrt$ to the right:
$$-2qrs + 10rst = -18qrt$$

steps.title: Factor s and divide
steps.content:
Factor $s$ from the left side:
$$s(-2qr + 10rt) = -18qrt$$
Divide both sides by $(-2qr + 10rt)$:
$$s = \frac{-18qrt}{-2qr + 10rt}$$

steps.title: Simplify by factoring the denominator
steps.content:
Factor $-2r(q - 5t)$ from the denominator:
$$s = \frac{-18qrt}{-2r(q - 5t)} = \frac{9qt}{q - 5t}$$
That confirms B is correct.
